replies.text:
OK, I understand that it was 15 years ago since they broke up, and they were literally teenagers when they met. But even if that’s the case, are you gonna literally going to tell me with a straight face that this was a smart move to make? 
Because usually, when it comes to break ups with celebrities, when they say it’s done and over, IT’S DONE AND OVER. I mean, if I had someone in my life, teenagers or even in my adult years, and if one of us comes to terms that this relationship is not working, therefore breaking up, I usually live by the idea that when it’s over, it’s over. No more contacting them, no we’re trying to get involved, let’s try to find our happy lives away from each other. 
So do you really think it’s a good idea to be literally after finally settling, moving along with your life, and finally marrying, to tell your wife, “Hey, honey! Sweetheart! I’m thinking about making a movie that we both can star in it together! Hey, I have another idea! Why don’t I cast my ex-girlfriend from 15 years ago in the role of this character? What do you say to that?”, and your wife is going to be all, “Gee, honey! That’s a great idea!”. NO. That is asking for drama, when there shouldn’t need to be drama.

Now again, I don’t know if any drama did occur on the set of making the film Sacramento. And even seeing a couple of press tours, and hearing Angarano, Stewart, and his actress wife Maya Erskine speak, it seems like everything went down cool. But understand that things like this could’ve turned out way differently. It’s a good thing nothing didn’t, but still.

replies.text:
Tbh, you CAN'T make it to the industry if you don't have connections. People who say otherwise are liars. I bet even Pattison's parents had a connection to some extent. Modelling agencies (where his mom's career is from) are not too far from Hollywood. 

However, what I find interesting about Pattinson is that he didn't let the backlash from Twilight dictate his career. Instead, he proved a lot of critics wrong by allowing his talent to speak for itself. 

Unlike Stewart, rather than desperately chasing blockbuster roles to maintain his relevance, he spent a little over five years in independent films, focusing on quality projects to solidify his career and possibly improve his acting skills after Twilight ended. When the time was right, he returned to blockbuster films. The results speak for themselves, especially considering he’s set to play in THREE  anticipated movies next year: The Odyssey by Nolan, *Dune 3 from Villeneuve, and a Drama with Zendaya. Well done

replies.author: @JYNDMB
replies.text:
​​@raijinkensama​ Apparently Vampire Diares was published in 1992 but had a resurgence bc of the popularity of the Twilight Series. Be it inspiration for Twilight or not, that's why there is some confusion of which inspired what one. 

That said, I can't find it bc it appears there's WAY MORE info about claims that don't seem as strong, at least to me, very readily available. I could've kept digging or maybe give AI prompts about vampire books (aside from Twilight) with a patriarchal character named Carlisle who was also a doctor. That's a detail I'm nearly positive about but there were certainly many other details for me to question plagiarism rather than just inspiration. Things like more of the names were repeated despite how unique they seem; Carlisle, Esme,and Jasper I want to say were all used. And even if it wasn't precisely 'Carlisle the doctor vampire was married to Esme and one of their adopted sons was Jasper', all those unique names in more than one vampire book? Not without any 'inspiration' going on, at the least. 
God it was long ago! I want to say my friend had found it herself by starting to read the other, pre-existing series. Probably looking for something similar to the Twilight series which I'm not even positive the final book of had been released yet. Then when she realized the similarities and looked further, she found the author had levied claims of plagiarism. Though, its quite possible it happened the other way- having heard of a series we enjoyed possibly being plagiarized then got the series it was allegedly ripped off from and finding it appeared true. 

I don't recall how much of the Twilight series seemed to be represented there but at least it's beginnings and when I just looked now, apparently there were questions surrounding Breaking Dawn's storyline. Even entire paragraphs looking awfully similar. So, perhaps she got her start for the series from a barely known series but tried to expand upon it and then still had to take from another author? What a shame. Getting your work out there isn't easy for authors without name recognition. Imagine watching someone breakthrough into a massive audience like Twilight had and it's your work?

author: @misscherryandcherry
text:
Kristen Stewart, the 1st American actress to win a César (the French Oscar), is also a BAFTA winner, an Oscar nominee, and has received numerous awards for her performance in Spencer, considered one of the best performances of the decade. She has also received numerous tribute awards from film awards and critics. She has worked with several respected and award-winning directors such as David Cronenberg, Pablo Larraín, Walter Salles, Olivier Assayas, and many others. She owns a production company and has produced, written, and directed numerous projects, including a short film that was screened at various festivals, including the Cannes Film Festival, one of the largest and most respected film festivals in the world, where Kristen Stewart has served on the jury and is always well-liked by the festival president and frequently involved with the films she's part of. 
She also served as president of the jury at the German film festival, Barlinale. And today she has a film adapted, written, produced, and directed by her independently - The Chronology of Water, which has one of the highest critical ratings of 2025 and has also been to international film festivals, such as Cannes, and has won several awards for both the film and her direction. 
And now she's also bought a movie theater; if that's what being in a bad career means, then I want to be that bad too.
